MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-11-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELLEN REITER'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DISMISSING, ABATING AND/OR STAYING PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOTIVE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DECLARATORY RELIEF COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 14, 2014, TO GIVE THE PARTIES AN OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH SAN FRANCISCO LOCAL RULE 2.6B. BOTH PARTIES ARE TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPIES OF THEIR FILINGS AND PROVIDE A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. THE COURT ONLY HAS A COURTESY COPY OF THE REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY, A CORPORATION ESTIMATED TIME FOR TRIAL: 2.0 DAYS
ORDER GRANTING DEFENDANT ELLEN REITTER'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DISMISSING ABATING AND OR STAYING PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOTIVE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DECLARATORY RELIEF COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-14-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ELLEN REITER'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER DISMISSING, ABATING AND/OR STAYING PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOTIVE INSURANCE COMPANY'S DECLARATORY RELIEF COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT REITER HAS MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT STATE FARM'S CLAIM FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF INVOLVES FACTUAL ISSUES THAT LIKELY OVERLAP WITH CASE CGC-12-518299 REGARDING WHETHER DEFENDANT SUFFERED ANY DAMAGES THAT WERE CAUSED BY THE USE OR OPERATION OF THE STATE FARM'S INSURED'S VEHICLE. (SEE GREAT AMERICAN INS. CO. V. SUP.CT. (ANGELES CHEM. CO., INC.) (2009) 178 CAL.APP.4TH 221, 233.) MOREOVER, THE UNDERLYING ACTION IS SCHEDULED FOR TRIAL IN ONE WEEK ON JANUARY 21, 2014. CONSEQUENTLY, THE PRESENT ACTION IS STAYED PENDING ADJUDICATION OF CASE CGC-12-518299. (SEE MONTROSE CHEM. CORP. OF CALIF. V. SUP.CT. (CANADIAN UNIVERSAL INS. CO., INC.) 6 CAL.4TH 287, 301-302.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF FEB-05-2014 CONTINUED TO APR-09-2014 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR STATUS OF STAY. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Sep-26-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-21-2014 Set For Trial On Oct-20-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-21-2014 Continued To Apr-09-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Dismissing, Abating And/Or Staying Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automotive Insurance Company'S Declaratory Relief Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-14-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ELLEN REITER'S Motion For An Order Dismissing, Abating And/Or Staying Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automotive Insurance Company'S Declaratory Relief Complaint is Granted. Defendant Reiter has made a sufficient showing that State Farm's claim for declaratory relief involves factual issues that likely overlap with case CGC-12-518299 regarding whether Defendant suffered any damages that were caused by the use or operation of the State Farm's insured's vehicle. (See Great American Ins. Co. v. Sup.Ct. (Angeles Chem. Co., Inc.) (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 221, 233.) Moreover, the underlying action is scheduled for trial in one week on January 21, 2014. Consequently, the present action is stayed pending adjudication of case CGC-12-518299. (See Montrose Chem. Corp. of Calif. v. Sup.Ct. (Canadian Universal Ins. Co., Inc.) 6 Cal.4th 287, 301-302.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Dismissing, Abating And/Or Staying Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automotive Insurance Company'S Declaratory Relief Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-11-2013 Continued To Jan-14-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ELLEN REITER'S Motion For An Order Dismissing, Abating And/Or Staying Plaintiff State Farm Mutual Automotive Insurance Company's Declaratory Relief Complaint is continued to January 14, 2014, to give the parties an opportunity to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Both parties are to provide courtesy copies of their filings and provide a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The Court only has a courtesy copy of the reply papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR #6185
